notes: Game runs natively after updating to the nVidia 430.14 drivers. Neon disco-like textures in the monuments. Runs at 30-50 FPS with minor stutters when looking around. Experienced a crash after about a hour of playtime but the game does seem playable for the most part. Does not boot past the main menu when enabling Proton 4.2-4
title: Rust

notes: Definitely playable. Randomly crashed while loading -- I reduced the settings graphical settings to 'potato' and it loaded right up within 2 minutes (I fixed the graphics after loading). Beyond this, some monument textures display as a spectrum of flashing colors when at a distance right after loading (this seems to go away?). Some lighting effects glare really hard as well. Otherwise the game runs way better than I expected considering all of the borked statuses.

notes: Runs good and performs comparably to Windows, except for the disco lighting effects on some textures.  What really brought the rating down was the random crashing.  Sometimes it could go hours without crashing, then two or three times in an hour.  I gave it a silver and not a bronze rating since I played 30+ hours on Linux before getting frustrated and booting up the Winblows partition to continue playing.  It appears to be a native install from the steam client, though it wouldn't launch until I enabled Steam Play, weird.  I haven't tried any of the tweaks, since it's native?
title: Rust

notes: The game work's in proton if you join a server through the console and don't click "play game", you just can't join an EAC secured server, the game run's great too.
title: Rust

notes: Can be played via Proton on servers which have disabled EAC and encryption. Framerate isn't quite as good as native, but it works and doesn't suffer from any major issues once you are connected to a server.

notes: I didn't test all of the tweaks. However, connecting to a custom server using the console (server list freezes the game!) works fine - but once you're ingame, lots of things will be transparent (terrain, house walls, horses, other creatures, players, ...) so the game is barely playable (if you're very tolerant).

notes: Follow-up to my previous reports - I noticed PROTON_USE_WINED3D helps on the visibility of things (not everything, but it's a decent start), at the cost of performance and some glitchy shaders on water. Still bronze for me - doesn't work on official servers and can't even display server list. Works fine on custom dedicated server without EAC however.
